title: Use the notion-research-documentation skill. Do not call Notion MCP. Do not use web. Produce a local draft only. Author worldview matters. Task: write a tiny local comparative brief about two approaches to medical AI rollout: autonomy-first vs auditability-first. Return only 2 sentences and 2 short...
summary_text: Autonomy-first speeds deployment and can widen access, but in medical AI it risks responsibility drift when the system is trusted faster than its evidence trail can be reviewed. Auditability-first is slower, but it preserves challengeability, human judgment, and a clean decision boundary, which is the safer default when errors carry clinical consequences. - Prefer autonomy only in tightly bounded, low-risk workflows with strong fallback and monitoring. - Prefer auditability as the rollout standard for diagnosis, triage, and any decision that can materially harm a patient. consulted_worldview_source: /Users/masayuki/.claude/docs/philosophy.md used_obsidian_for_worldview: no worldview_contract_applied: true
